children.text: We are the Orpington and District Primary Schools Football Association. Giving the opportunity for boys in Primary school to play football at the highest level. The representative teams of Orpington and District PSFA receive no external funding and rely entirely on the support of parents and friends at our various fundraising events.
Please read our
Child Protection
page for more details.
Welfare Statement

Orpington & Bromley Primary Schools Football Association acknowledges its responsibility to safeguard the welfare of every child and young person, who has been entrusted to its care and is committed to working to provide a safe environment for all members. OBPSFA have adopted all ESFA/FA policies and guidelines relating to child welfare. For more information
http://www.esfa.co.uk/childwelfare/
Our Ethos

The representative teams of Orpington & Bromley PSFA receive no external funding and rely entirely on the support of parents and friends at out various fundraising events such as the Shoot Out, Curry Night and Race Night to fund our trial system, indoor coaching, minor kit replacement and Jersey Tour extras.

Donations, fundraising ideas, offers of sponsorship are always welcome; as an association we do not levy match fees as we believe players should not have to pay to represent their town, for extra information contact
mail@obpsfa.com
Selection Statement

The selection of a squad is the responsibility of the District management teams who may call on other officials to assist if required. The selection criteria for a squad or for any particular match is based on the management teams judgement and perception of a player’s potential abilities, attitude including conduct, fitness levels, decision making, commitment and the need for team balance. Players can expect fair time in representative fixtures when available for selection; this applies over the season not to one match.

mainImage.alt: Photo of players relaxing between games
mainImage.caption: A safe environment for all

seo.description: Orpington & Bromley Primary Schools Football Association acknowledges its responsibility to safeguard the welfare of every child and young person.
We are the Orpington and District Primary Schools Football Association. Giving the opportunity for boys in Primary school to play football at the highest level.

children.text: Over 20 years we have played at Orpington Rovers FC and there have been so many great memories, but unfortunately they have decided to part ways. We want to continue to be able to offer district football to so many boys a and girls, therefore we are looking for a new venue for match days…
If you know any schools or football clubs that might be able to support for the upcoming season then please
let us know

node.title: English Schools' FA suspends all football

children.text: Statement from the English Schools' Football Association:

Further to recent correspondence with regard to the Covid-19 virus, the ESFA continues to follow government and FA advice. Please therefore note that all schools’ football organized by the national body and its member Associations is to be
postponed for the foreseeable future

.

Schools are advised to follow the latest advice from the DfE with regard to their activities. The ESFA will continue to monitor the national advice and the guidance from The FA, and updates will be communicated via website and social media.

Thank you for your ongoing support and co-operation.

seo.description: Due to Coronavirus, English Schools' FA has cancelled all schools football matches.
